drafts:
**Pattern:** ^enum\s+\w+\s*\{[^}]*\}(?!\s*##\s*APPEND ONLY)
**Why:** Godot serializes @export enum fields as raw integers, not names. Inserting a member before an existing one silently reclassifies all fixtures that serialize higher-index values with no error or warning. Each enum must carry an APPEND ONLY guard comment.

**Pattern:** `debug_assert!`
**Why:** Reviewer required `assert!` instead of `debug_assert!` in simulation/library code so that non-finite floating-point values are caught in release builds, preventing silent propagation of corrupt data and system desynchronization.

```yaml
language: rust
rule:
  pattern: |
    fn spawn_one_agent($$$, speed_multiplier: f32, $$$) -> Entity {
      $$$
    }
```
**Why:** Reviewer required a runtime `assert!(speed_multiplier.is_finite() && speed_multiplier > 0.0, ...)` guard at the spawn boundary to catch non-finite or non-positive multiplier values loudly rather than silently propagating corrupt data.

**Pattern:** `assert_copy::<SpeedMultiplier>`
**Why:** Reviewer required every new ECS component to be listed in the compile-time DoD invariant block (`assert_copy::<T>()`) in `components.rs` to guarantee all components remain `Copy` and cache-friendly. Absence of the assertion means the invariant is not enforced for the new component.

**Pattern:** `\.\.(=?)SPEED_MULTIPLIER_MAX`
**Why:** Reviewer noted that `jitter_f32` produces a half-open range `[MIN, MAX)`, so bounds-check assertions must use the exclusive range `..SPEED_MULTIPLIER_MAX` (not `..=SPEED_MULTIPLIER_MAX`). Using the inclusive form makes the upper-bound check vacuously true and the error message misleading.

**Pattern:** func setup_player_proxy[^:]*:\s*\n(?!\s*(var existing|if _player_3d_proxy\.has_node))
**Why:** `setup_player_proxy` must begin with an idempotency guard (check for an existing "Visual" child) before adding a new child to `_player_3d_proxy`; on scene-tree re-entry `_ready()` re-fires and unconditionally adding a child stacks duplicate visuals and leaks the previous subtree.

**Pattern:** packed\.instantiate\(\)\s+as\s+Node3D(?![\s\S]{0,200}push_error)
**Why:** If `packed.instantiate() as Node3D` returns null (GLB scene root is not a Node3D subtype), the code silently falls through to the BoxMesh stub with no diagnostic; a `push_error` must be emitted on the null-cast path to mirror the file-not-found handling and keep the fallback observable.

**Pattern:** (?:const|var)\s+\w*(?:OFFSET|SPEED|SCALE|WEIGHT|FORCE|MASS|RADIUS|HEIGHT|DISTANCE|DURATION|RATE|FACTOR|THRESHOLD|LIMIT|RANGE|ANGLE|POWER|ENERGY|HEALTH|DAMAGE|COST|TIME|DELAY|INTERVAL|PERIOD|FREQUENCY|AMPLITUDE|MAGNITUDE|INTENSITY|LEVEL|SCORE|COUNT|SIZE|WIDTH|HEIGHT|DEPTH|LENGTH|VOLUME|AREA|DENSITY|PRESSURE|TEMPERATURE|VELOCITY|ACCELERATION|FRICTION|BOUNCE|GRAVITY|DRAG|LIFT|TORQUE|INERTIA|MOMENTUM|IMPULSE|TENSION|STIFFNESS|DAMPING|RESTITUTION|ELASTICITY|VISCOSITY|CONDUCTIVITY|CAPACITY|EFFICIENCY|RATIO|MULTIPLIER|MODIFIER|BONUS|PENALTY|REWARD|COST|PRICE|VALUE|AMOUNT|QUANTITY|BUDGET|LIMIT|CAP|MAX|MIN|BASE|DEFAULT|INITIAL|FINAL|TARGET|GOAL|THRESHOLD|TOLERANCE|MARGIN|BUFFER|OFFSET|BIAS|GAIN|LOSS|DELTA|STEP|INCREMENT|DECREMENT|RATE|SPEED|VELOCITY|ACCELERATION|JERK|SNAP|CRACKLE|POP)\w*\s*(?::|=)\s*[-+]?[0-9]+(?:\.[0-9]+)?\b
**Why:** Gameplay and presentation tunables (numeric constants such as offsets, speeds, scales, etc.) must not be hardcoded in GDScript files; they must live in `.tres` resources or configuration files per Repository Style Guide §5, §6, and Tenet 3.

**Pattern:** `assert!\s*\(.*\.is_finite\(\)` is ABSENT in functions that cast float to u8/u16/u32 after arithmetic
**Why:** Reviewer required runtime `assert!` checks for finiteness (NaN/Inf) on floating-point inputs before integer casts to prevent silent propagation of corrupt data and system desynchronization.

```yaml
language: rust
rule:
  pattern: $X as u8
  not:
    precedes:
      pattern: assert!($X.is_finite())
```
**Why:** Any float-to-u8 cast must be preceded by a finiteness assertion; truncation of NaN/Inf produces silent corrupt values.

**Pattern:** `varying\s+flat\b`
**Why:** In Godot 4 shading language the interpolation qualifier `flat` must precede `varying` (i.e. `flat varying`). The order `varying flat` causes a shader compilation error.

**Pattern:** `_multimesh\.instance_count\s*=\s*0`
**Why:** Setting `instance_count = 0` unconditionally before checking current state causes a one-frame blank flash on repeated enable calls. An idempotency guard (early-return when requested state already matches) must precede any MultiMesh reset.

**Pattern:** `debug_assert!\s*\(.*\.is_finite\(\)`
**Why:** Reviewer required `assert!` (not `debug_assert!`) for finite-float checks in simulation/library code so that NaN/Inf values are caught in release builds and cannot silently propagate to FFI buffers or the presentation layer.
